1	// Package triage provides the post-attempt decision module that the
2	// drain loop consults after each terminal event. Given a bead's failure
3	// mode and the prior modes seen in this bead's attempt history, the
4	// policy returns the next action to take (re-attempt, escalate, file
5	// follow-up, etc.).
6	//
7	// This package is the standalone policy core; loop integration lands in
8	// a follow-up bead.
9	package triage
10	
11	import "fmt"
12	
13	// FailureMode classifies the terminal event of a single execute-bead attempt.
14	type FailureMode string
15	
16	const (
17		// FailureModeReviewBlock — reviewer returned BLOCK on the committed result.
18		FailureModeReviewBlock FailureMode = "review_block"
19		// FailureModeLockContention — claim/lock could not be acquired.
20		FailureModeLockContention FailureMode = "lock_contention"
21		// FailureModeExecutionFailed — runner crashed, harness errored, or
22		// timed out without producing a usable commit.
23		FailureModeExecutionFailed FailureMode = "execution_failed"
24		// FailureModeNoChanges — attempt completed without producing a commit.
25		FailureModeNoChanges FailureMode = "no_changes"
26	)
27	
28	// Action is the decision the policy returns for an attempt.
29	type Action string
30	
31	const (
32		// ActionReAttemptWithContext — re-queue the bead with the prior
33		// failure context threaded into the next prompt.
34		ActionReAttemptWithContext Action = "re_attempt_with_context"
35		// ActionEscalateTier — re-queue with a higher-capability model tier.
36		ActionEscalateTier Action = "escalate_tier"
37		// ActionRetryWithBackoff — retry after a delay (lock contention etc.).
38		ActionRetryWithBackoff Action = "retry_with_backoff"
39		// ActionFileFollowup — file a follow-up bead and close this one.
40		ActionFileFollowup Action = "file_followup"
41		// ActionNeedsHuman — park the bead for manual triage.
42		ActionNeedsHuman Action = "needs_human"
43	)
44	
45	// TriagePolicy maps a FailureMode to an ordered ladder of Actions. The
46	// nth time a given mode is seen in a bead's history, the nth rung of
47	// its ladder is returned. Past the end of the ladder, the final rung
48	// (typically ActionNeedsHuman) is returned.
49	type TriagePolicy struct {
50		Ladders map[FailureMode][]Action
51	}
52	
53	// DefaultPolicy returns the built-in policy. Mirrors the parent bead's
54	// AC#3: BLOCK → re_attempt_with_context, escalate_tier, needs_human.
55	func DefaultPolicy() TriagePolicy {
56		return TriagePolicy{Ladders: map[FailureMode][]Action{
57			FailureModeReviewBlock: {
58				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
59				ActionEscalateTier,
60				ActionNeedsHuman,
61			},
62			FailureModeLockContention: {
63				ActionRetryWithBackoff,
64				ActionRetryWithBackoff,
65				ActionNeedsHuman,
66			},
67			FailureModeExecutionFailed: {
68				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
69				ActionEscalateTier,
70				ActionNeedsHuman,
71			},
72			FailureModeNoChanges: {
73				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
74				ActionFileFollowup,
75				ActionNeedsHuman,
76			},
77		}}
78	}
79	
80	// Decide returns the next Action for an attempt that ended in `mode`,
81	// given the ordered list of failure modes already seen for this bead
82	// (most recent last; the current attempt is NOT included).
83	//
84	// beadID is accepted for future per-bead policy customisation and to
85	// keep call sites stable; the default policy ignores it.
86	func (p TriagePolicy) Decide(beadID string, mode FailureMode, history []FailureMode) Action {
87		ladder := p.Ladders[mode]
88		if len(ladder) == 0 {
89			return ActionNeedsHuman
90		}
91		n := 0
92		for _, m := range history {
93			if m == mode {
94				n++
95			}
96		}
97		if n >= len(ladder) {
98			return ladder[len(ladder)-1]
99		}
100		return ladder[n]
101	}
102	
103	// ParseAction validates and converts a string from config into an Action.
104	func ParseAction(s string) (Action, error) {
105		switch Action(s) {
106		case ActionReAttemptWithContext,
107			ActionEscalateTier,
108			ActionRetryWithBackoff,
109			ActionFileFollowup,
110			ActionNeedsHuman:
111			return Action(s), nil
112		default:
113			return "", fmt.Errorf("triage: unknown action %q", s)
114		}
115	}
116	
117	// ParseFailureMode validates and converts a string from config into a FailureMode.
118	func ParseFailureMode(s string) (FailureMode, error) {
119		switch FailureMode(s) {
120		case FailureModeReviewBlock,
121			FailureModeLockContention,
122			FailureModeExecutionFailed,
123			FailureModeNoChanges:
124			return FailureMode(s), nil
125		default:
126			return "", fmt.Errorf("triage: unknown failure mode %q", s)
127		}
128	}

    Foundation for the auto-triage feature. Adds:
    
    - cli/internal/triage: standalone TriagePolicy with FailureMode and
      Action enums, and a Decide(beadID, mode, history) method whose
      default ladder for review_block matches the parent AC: re-attempt →
      escalate_tier → needs_human. Sensible defaults for the other modes.
    - Top-level triage: block in .ddx/config.yaml schema and NewConfig,
      surfaced on ResolvedConfig via TriagePolicy(). Optional; absent
      config falls back to the default policy.
    - Unit tests: ladder progression for review_block, defaults for
      other modes, schema accept/reject paths, and the config-override
      path on Resolve().

5	// TestFailureModeClassifyWorker exercises ClassifyFailureMode against the
6	// known worker-level signals. The acceptance contract enumerates the full
7	// set of modes: context_overflow, merge_conflict, test_failure,
8	// build_failure, timeout, auth_error, no_changes, unknown (plus "" on
9	// success). Each case names its input so a pattern drift is easy to spot
10	// in test failures.
11	func TestFailureModeClassifyWorker(t *testing.T) {
12		cases := []struct {
13			name     string
14			outcome  string
15			exitCode int
16			errMsg   string
17			want     string
18		}{
19			{"success_clean", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded, 0, "", ""},
20			{"no_changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges, 0, "", FailureModeNoChanges},
21			{"no_evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence, 0, "", FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced},
22			{"no_changes_ignores_error_text", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges, 0, "build failed", FailureModeNoChanges},
23	
24			// Context / long-context hangs.
25			{"context_length_exceeded", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
26				"context_length_exceeded: prompt exceeds 200000 tokens", FailureModeContextOverflow},
27			{"prompt_too_long", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
28				"Error: prompt is too long for model", FailureModeContextOverflow},
29			{"maximum_context", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
30				"request exceeds the maximum context length", FailureModeContextOverflow},
31	
32			// Auth / quota.
33			{"unauthorized_401", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
34				"401 Unauthorized: invalid API key", FailureModeAuthError},
35			{"insufficient_quota", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
36				"insufficient_quota: you have exceeded your current quota", FailureModeAuthError},
37			{"rate_limit", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
38				"429 Too Many Requests: rate limit reached", FailureModeAuthError},
39	
40			// Timeout.
41			{"context_deadline_exceeded", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
42				"context deadline exceeded", FailureModeTimeout},
43			{"timed_out", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
44				"agent call timed out after 2h", FailureModeTimeout},
45	
46			// Merge conflict (worker-visible when orchestrator surfaces it via
47			// Reason/Error). The landing classifier refines this; the worker
48			// classifier recognises the text so a pre-landing result is still
49			// tagged correctly.
50			{"merge_conflict", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
51				"merge conflict in cli/cmd/agent_metrics.go", FailureModeMergeConflict},
52	
53			// Build failure: shipped code that does not compile.
54			{"go_compile_error", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
55				"undefined: foo.Bar", FailureModeBuildFailure},
56			{"build_failed", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
57				"build failed with 3 errors", FailureModeBuildFailure},
58	
59			// Test failure.
60			{"go_test_fail_marker", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,

62			{"tests_failed", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
63				"tests failed: 3 of 12 assertions did not pass", FailureModeTestFailure},
64	
65			// Unknown fallback: non-zero exit with no recognised pattern.
66			{"unknown_error", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
67				"something exploded", FailureModeUnknown},
68			{"no_error_text_non_zero", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 137, "",
69				FailureModeUnknown},
70	
71			// Ordering: timeout wins over test/build keywords when both appear.
72			{"timeout_beats_test_failure", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
73				"test timed out after 5m", FailureModeTimeout},
74			// Ordering: auth wins over generic keywords.
75			{"auth_beats_build", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
76				"401 unauthorized: build failed", FailureModeAuthError},

148	// TestFailureModeApplyLandingToResult verifies that ApplyLandingToResult
149	// writes the refined failure_mode onto the result, giving callers a single
150	// unified record. This is the contract callers of result.json depend on.
151	func TestFailureModeApplyLandingToResult(t *testing.T) {
152		// Worker recorded a context overflow; landing then merged the result
153		// cleanly (unlikely but possible if the worker bounced mid-run and a
154		// retry landed). The landing decision wins: failure_mode clears.
155		res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
156			BeadID:      "ddx-test",
157			BaseRev:     "a",
158			ResultRev:   "b",
159			ExitCode:    0,
160			Outcome:     ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded,
161			FailureMode: FailureModeContextOverflow, // carried from a prior attempt
162		}
163		ApplyLandingToResult(res, &BeadLandingResult{Outcome: "merged"})
164		if res.FailureMode != "" {
165			t.Fatalf("merged landing should clear FailureMode, got %q", res.FailureMode)
166		}
167	
168		// Worker succeeded; landing could not merge. FailureMode becomes
169		// merge_conflict so measurement can bucket the failure.
170		res = &ExecuteBeadResult{
171			BeadID:      "ddx-test",
172			BaseRev:     "a",
173			ResultRev:   "b",
174			ExitCode:    0,
175			Outcome:     ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded,
176			FailureMode: "",
177		}
178		ApplyLandingToResult(res, &BeadLandingResult{Outcome: "preserved", Reason: "merge conflict"})
179		if res.FailureMode != FailureModeMergeConflict {
180			t.Fatalf("merge conflict landing should set merge_conflict, got %q", res.FailureMode)
181		}
182	
183		// Worker succeeded; gate failed with a build diagnostic in stderr.
184		res = &ExecuteBeadResult{
185			BeadID:    "ddx-test",
186			BaseRev:   "a",
187			ResultRev: "b",
188			ExitCode:  0,
189			Outcome:   ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded,
190		}
191		ApplyLandingToResult(res, &BeadLandingResult{
192			Outcome: "preserved",
193			Reason:  "post-run checks failed",
194			GateResults: []GateCheckResult{{
195				DefinitionID: "go-build",
196				Required:     true,
197				Status:       "fail",
198				Stderr:       "undefined: foo.Bar",
199			}},
200		})
201		if res.FailureMode != FailureModeBuildFailure {
202			t.Fatalf("build-diagnostic gate failure should set build_failure, got %q", res.FailureMode)
203		}
204	
205		// No-changes landing outcome always maps to no_changes regardless of
206		// what the worker recorded.
207		res = &ExecuteBeadResult{
208			BeadID:      "ddx-test",
209			BaseRev:     "a",
210			ResultRev:   "a",
211			ExitCode:    0,
212			Outcome:     ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges,
213			FailureMode: FailureModeNoChanges,
214		}
